```{python}
#| label: setup-brand
#| include: false

import os, json, pathlib
import matplotlib as mpl
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.font_manager as fm
import pyfonts

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from great_tables import GT, loc, style
from scipy.stats import beta as beta_dist


def get_brand_info() -> dict:
    raw = os.environ.get("QUARTO_EXECUTE_INFO", "")
    if not raw:
        return {}
    p = pathlib.Path(raw)
    try:
        return json.loads(p.read_text() if p.is_file() else raw)
    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
        return {}


def configure_brand_fonts() -> None:
    info = get_brand_info()
    brand = info.get("format", {}).get("render", {}).get("brand", {})
    doc_dir = pathlib.Path(info.get("document-path", "")).parent

    for mode in ["light", "dark"]:
        if mode not in brand:
            continue
        fonts = brand[mode].get("data", {}).get("typography", {}).get("fonts", [])
        for font_spec in fonts:
            family = font_spec.get("family", "")
            source = font_spec.get("source", "")
            if not family or not source or source == "system":
                continue
            if source == "file":
                for f in font_spec.get("files", []):
                    resolved = doc_dir / f.get("path", "")
                    if resolved.exists():
                        fm.fontManager.addfont(str(resolved))
            elif source in ("bunny", "google"):
                loader = pyfonts.load_bunny_font if source == "bunny" else pyfonts.load_google_font
                fm.fontManager.addfont(loader(family).get_file())


def _col_mix(a: str, b: str, amount: float = 0.25) -> str:
    """Blend two hex colours. amount=0 → a, amount=1 → b."""
    ra, ga, ba = int(a[1:3], 16), int(a[3:5], 16), int(a[5:7], 16)
    rb, gb, bb = int(b[1:3], 16), int(b[3:5], 16), int(b[5:7], 16)
    r = int(ra + (rb - ra) * amount)
    g = int(ga + (gb - ga) * amount)
    b_val = int(ba + (bb - ba) * amount)
    return f"#{r:02x}{g:02x}{b_val:02x}"


def _get_brand_colors(brand_mode: str = "light") -> dict:
    """Return the processedData.color dict for a given mode — all hex, no lookups."""
    info = get_brand_info()
    return (
        info.get("format", {})
        .get("render", {})
        .get("brand", {})
        .get(brand_mode, {})
        .get("processedData", {})
        .get("color", {})
    )


def _get_brand_typography(brand_mode: str = "light") -> dict:
    info = get_brand_info()
    return (
        info.get("format", {})
        .get("render", {})
        .get("brand", {})
        .get(brand_mode, {})
        .get("data", {})
        .get("typography", {})
    )


def get_brand_palette(brand_mode: str = "light") -> list[str]:
    info = get_brand_info()
    palette = (
        info.get("format", {})
        .get("render", {})
        .get("brand", {})
        .get(brand_mode, {})
        .get("data", {})
        .get("color", {})
        .get("palette", {})
    )
    return list(palette.values())


def get_chart_colors(brand_mode: str | None = None) -> dict:
    """Return a colour-token dict for charts.

    Reads the active rendering mode from QUARTO_EXECUTE_INFO automatically.
    All semantic keys come from processedData.color — already resolved hex.
    """
    info = get_brand_info()
    mode = brand_mode or info.get("rendering", "light")
    colors = _get_brand_colors(mode)

    ink   = colors.get("foreground", "#1C1917")
    paper = colors.get("background", "#FAFAF9")

    return {
        "mode":    mode,
        "hero":    colors.get("primary", ink),
        "annot":   colors.get("warning", ink),
        "text":    ink,
        "paper":   paper,
        "accent":  _col_mix(ink, paper, 0.25),
        "muted":   _col_mix(ink, paper, 0.60),
        "sub":     _col_mix(ink, paper, 0.45),
        "grid":    _col_mix(ink, paper, 0.85),
        "palette": get_brand_palette(mode),
    }


def apply_brand_theme(mode: str | None = None, base_size: int = 10) -> None:
    """Apply brand colours and fonts to matplotlib via plt.rcParams.update().

    Reads the active rendering mode from QUARTO_EXECUTE_INFO when mode is None.
    Sets figure and savefig backgrounds to the brand paper colour so PNGs never
    render with a white background under the Agg backend.
    """
    info = get_brand_info()
    active_mode = mode or info.get("rendering", "light")
    colors = _get_brand_colors(active_mode)
    typo   = _get_brand_typography(active_mode)

    ink   = colors.get("foreground", "#1C1917")
    paper = colors.get("background", "#FAFAF9")
    base_family = typo.get("base", "sans-serif")

    plt.rcParams.update({
        "font.family":          base_family,
        "font.size":            base_size,
        "figure.facecolor":     paper,
        "axes.facecolor":       "none",
        "savefig.facecolor":    paper,
        "savefig.transparent":  False,
        "text.color":           ink,
        "axes.labelcolor":      ink,
        "xtick.color":          _col_mix(ink, paper, 0.45),
        "ytick.color":          _col_mix(ink, paper, 0.45),
        "axes.spines.top":      False,
        "axes.spines.right":    False,
        "axes.spines.left":     False,
        "axes.spines.bottom":   False,
        "axes.grid":            False,
        "grid.color":           _col_mix(ink, paper, 0.85),
        "grid.linewidth":       0.5,
        "xtick.bottom":         False,
        "ytick.left":           False,
        "axes.prop_cycle":      mpl.cycler(color=get_brand_palette(active_mode) or [ink]),
    })


def gt_brand(data, brand_mode: str = "light") -> GT:
    """Style a great_tables GT using processedData.color — all hex, no lookups."""
    colors  = _get_brand_colors(brand_mode)
    typo    = _get_brand_typography(brand_mode)
    palette = get_brand_palette(brand_mode)

    if not colors:
        return GT(data)

    ink   = colors.get("foreground", "#1C1917")
    paper = colors.get("background", "#FAFAF9")
    accent = _col_mix(ink, paper, 0.25)
    base_family    = typo.get("base", "")
    heading_family = typo.get("headings", base_family)

    tbl = GT(data)
    tbl = tbl.tab_options(
        table_font_names=base_family,
        table_font_color=ink,
        table_background_color=paper,
        column_labels_background_color=palette[0] if palette else accent,
        column_labels_font_weight="bold",
        table_body_border_top_color=accent,
        table_body_border_bottom_color=accent,
    )
    tbl = tbl.tab_style(
        style=style.text(font=heading_family, color=paper, weight="bold"),
        locations=loc.column_labels(),
    )
    return tbl


configure_brand_fonts()
```

```{python}
#| label: debug-brand-colors-light
#| eval: false
import pprint
pprint.pprint(_get_brand_colors("light"))
```

```{python}
#| label: debug-brand-colors-dark
#| eval: false
import pprint
pprint.pprint(_get_brand_colors("dark"))
```
